body:
Closes #3285

This is a big API change so we'll have to bump a minor version.

This PR changes the behavior of the internal function `node`, which returns a tuple of coordinates at index `i, j, k` and location `lx, ly, lz`, to omit coordinates from the tuple along `Flat` directions. Previously, `node` omitted coordinates only for `Nothing` locations. Now, `Flat` directions are treated similarly to `Nothing` locations.

This change changes the API for using `set!` with functions, and therefore for setting initial conditions, building immersed boundaries, using boundary condition functions, and background fields.

Before this PR, one might write

```julia
grid = RectilinearGrid(size=64, z=(0, 1), topology=(Flat, Flat, Grid))
c = CenterField(grid)
random_numbers(x, y, z) = rand()
set!(c, random_numbers)
```

After this PR, this code has to change because the `x, y` directions are `Flat`. Thus after this PR we will write

```julia
grid = RectilinearGrid(size=64, z=(0, 1), topology=(Flat, Flat, Grid))
c = CenterField(grid)
random_numbers(z) = rand()
set!(c, random_numbers)
```

As a rule, coordinates within functions should never be provided for `Flat` directions or for `Nothing` locations.

There's more discussion about the pros and cons of this choice on #3285.
title: (0.90.0) Omit entries in Flat direction from `node`
